02A. Maternal Effects and Reproduction: Egg count

Elliott Schmidt

2026-04-11

Load libraries

library(tidyverse) # data manipulation
library(ggpubr) # producing data exploratory plots
library(modelsummary) # descriptive data 
library(glmmTMB) # running generalised mixed models 
library(DHARMa) # model diagnostics 
library(performance) # model diagnostics  
library(ggeffects) # partial effect plots 
library(car) # running Anova on model 
library(emmeans) # post-hoc analysis  
library(MuMIn) # model comparisons
library(lme4)  #blups
library(patchwork) #aligning plots

Import data

setwd("C:/Users/elliott/OneDrive/jc527762/PhD dissertation/Data/Chapter3_Heritability_ARCHIVED")
egg <- read_csv("import_data/egg_size_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:14,factor))  |> 
  select(!c(NOTES,...18, IMAGE))   

reprod.data <- read_csv("import_data/clutch_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(c(1:7,16,23), factor))  
  

m2.5 <- read_csv("import_data/2-5_month_size_data_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:15,factor)) |> 
  mutate(STANDARD_LENGTH =LENGTH, 
         .keep = "unused") |> 
  select(!(NOTES)) |> 
  select(1:15,"STANDARD_LENGTH","MASS")|> 
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(DENSITY = n()) |> 
  ungroup()

adult <- read_csv("import_data/adult_size_2022_2023.csv") |> 
  mutate(across(1:3,factor), 
         MALE = FISH_ID, 
         FEMALE = FISH_ID, 
         POPULATION = str_sub(FISH_ID, 2,4), 
         POPULATION = case_when(POPULATION == "ARL" ~ "Arlington Reef", 
                                POPULATION == "SUD" ~ "Sudbury Reef",
                                POPULATION == "VLA" ~ "Vlassof cay",
                                POPULATION == "PRE" ~ "Pretty patches", 
                                TRUE ~ POPULATION)) |> 
  left_join(select(m2.5, c("MALE","TEMPERATURE")), 
             by="MALE") |> 
  left_join(select(m2.5, c("FEMALE","TEMPERATURE")), 
             by="FEMALE") |>
  distinct() |> 
  mutate(TEMPERATURE = coalesce(TEMPERATURE.x, TEMPERATURE.y)) |> 
  drop_na(TEMPERATURE) |> 
  select(-c("TEMPERATURE.x","TEMPERATURE.y"))

Data manipulation

m2.5_df_all <- m2.5 |> 
  left_join(select(adult, c("MALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="MALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_MALE =SL, 
         MASS_MALE =MASS.y, 
         .keep = "unused") |>
  left_join(select(adult, c("FEMALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="FEMALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_FEMALE =SL, 
         MASS_FEMALE =MASS, 
         .keep ="unused") |> 
  mutate(SL_MIDPOINT = (SL_MALE+SL_FEMALE)/2, 
         MASS_MIDPOINT = (MASS_MALE+MASS_FEMALE)/2) 

m2.5_df <- m2.5_df_all |>
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(MEDIAN_STANDARD_LENGTH = median(STANDARD_LENGTH)) |>
  drop_na(MEDIAN_STANDARD_LENGTH) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(-c("STANDARD_LENGTH","MASS.x")) |> 
  distinct() |> 
  mutate(MASS_MIDPOINT =coalesce(MASS_MIDPOINT, MASS_MALE), 
         SL_MIDPOINT =coalesce(SL_MIDPOINT, SL_MALE)) 

clutches <- reprod.data |> filter(PROJECT_CODE %in% c("1","3","5","7")) |>
  select(CLUTCH_NUMBER)

egg_df_all <- egg |> 
  left_join(select(adult, c("MALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="MALE")  |> 
  mutate(SL_MALE =SL, 
         MASS_MALE =MASS, 
         .keep = "unused") |>
  left_join(select(adult, c("FEMALE", "SL", "MASS")), 
            by ="FEMALE") |> 
  mutate(SL_FEMALE =SL, 
         MASS_FEMALE =MASS, 
         .keep ="unused") |> 
  mutate(SL_MIDPOINT = (SL_MALE+SL_FEMALE)/2, 
         MASS_MIDPOINT = (MASS_MALE+MASS_FEMALE)/2) |> 
  left_join(select(reprod.data, c("CLUTCH_NUMBER","EGG_COUNT","HATCHING_SUCCESS")), by="CLUTCH_NUMBER")

egg_df <- egg_df_all |>
  group_by(CLUTCH_NUMBER) |> 
  mutate(MEDIAN_EGG_COUNT = median(EGG_COUNT), 
         MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE =median(EGG_SIZE)) |> 
  drop_na(MEDIAN_EGG_COUNT) |>
  ungroup()  |> 
  select(-c("EGG_SIZE","SAMPLE")) |> 
  distinct() |> 
  mutate(MASS_MIDPOINT =coalesce(MASS_MIDPOINT, MASS_MALE), 
         SL_MIDPOINT =coalesce(SL_MIDPOINT, SL_MALE), 
         SL_FEMALE =coalesce(SL_FEMALE, SL_MALE))  # done for two individuals
  
egg_df <- clutches |> 
  inner_join(egg_df, by="CLUTCH_NUMBER") |> 
  droplevels()

Exploratory data analysis

plot1 <- ggplot(egg_df, aes(x=MASS_FEMALE, y=MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, color=TEMPERATURE)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.05) + 
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", se=FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  ggtitle("EGG SIZE") +
  theme_classic()

plot2 <- ggplot(egg_df, aes(x=MASS_FEMALE, y=EGG_COUNT, color=TEMPERATURE)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.05) + 
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", se=FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  ggtitle("EGG COUNT") +
  theme_classic() 

plot3 <- ggplot(egg_df, aes(x=as.numeric(DAYS_IN_TREATMENT), y=EGG_COUNT, color=TEMPERATURE)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.05) + 
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", se=FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  ggtitle("EGG COUNT") +
  theme_classic()

ggarrange(plot1, plot2, 
          nrow=1, 
          ncol=2, 
          common.legend = TRUE)

Descriptive statistics

datasummary(Factor(POPULATION) ~ Factor(TEMPERATURE), 
            data=egg_df, 
            fmt = "%.0f")
POPULATION 27 28.5 30
Arlington Reef 9 6 8
Pretty Patches 4 3 5
Sudbury Reef 4 2 3
Vlassof Cay 4 2 4 
datasummary(Factor(TEMPERATURE) ~ EGG_COUNT * (NUnique + mean + median + min + max + sd + Histogram), 
            data = drop_na(egg_df),  
            fmt = "%.2f")
TEMPERATURE NUnique mean median min max sd Histogram
27 17 481.82 486.00 326.00 689.00 104.40 ▁▄▄▁▇▃▁▁
28.5 12 487.17 463.00 328.00 758.00 113.65 ▅▇▇▇▂
30 16 475.00 494.00 304.00 580.00 84.38 ▁▃▁▁▇▃▃▄ 
sample_size_eggcount <- egg_df |> 
  group_by(TEMPERATURE, MALE, FEMALE, POPULATION, CLUTCH_NUMBER, EGG_COUNT) |> 
  summarise(n =n(), .groups = "drop"); sample_size_eggcount
write.csv(sample_size_eggcount, "../../sample_size_eggcount.csv")

Fit models [random factors]

modelNULL <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ 1, 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data =reprod.data)

model1 <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ (1|CLUTCH_ORDER), 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data = reprod.data)

model2 <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ (1|POPULATION), 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data = reprod.data)  
## Warning in finalizeTMB(TMBStruc, obj, fit, h, data.tmb.old): Model convergence
## problem; non-positive-definite Hessian matrix. See vignette('troubleshooting')
model3 <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ (1|FEMALE), 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data = reprod.data)

model4 <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ (1|FEMALE) + (1|POPULATION), 
                  family=gaussian(),
                  data = reprod.data)
## Warning in finalizeTMB(TMBStruc, obj, fit, h, data.tmb.old): Model convergence
## problem; non-positive-definite Hessian matrix. See vignette('troubleshooting')
AIC(modelNULL, model1, model2, model3, model4, k=3) 
BIC(modelNULL, model1, model2, model3, model4)

Fit fixed factors

Main hypothesis

model1a <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ scale(MASS_FEMALE, center=TRUE)*TEMPERATURE + scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center=TRUE) + (1|FEMALE), 
                    family=gaussian(), 
                    data=egg_df)

Alternative hypothesis

model1b <- glmmTMB(EGG_COUNT ~ scale(MASS_FEMALE, center=TRUE)*TEMPERATURE + scale(as.numeric(DAYS_IN_TREATMENT), center=TRUE) + scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center=TRUE) +  (1|FEMALE), 
                    family=gaussian(), 
                    data=egg_df)

Model selection

AICc(model1a, model1b, k=5)

Model validation

DHARMa

model1a |> 
  simulateResiduals(plot=TRUE)  

## Object of Class DHARMa with simulated residuals based on 250 simulations with refit = FALSE . See ?DHARMa::simulateResiduals for help. 
##  
## Scaled residual values: 0.384 0.216 0.936 0.964 0.676 0.676 0.812 0.584 0.16 0.312 0.624 0.224 0.248 0.356 0.452 0.748 0.644 0.968 0.044 0.876 ...
model1a |> testResiduals(plot=T) 

## $uniformity
## 
##  Asymptotic one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
## D = 0.096, p-value = 0.7461
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
## 
## 
## $dispersion
## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## dispersion = 1.0662, p-value = 0.704
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
## 
## 
## $outliers
## 
##  DHARMa outlier test based on exact binomial test with approximate
##  expectations
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## outliers at both margin(s) = 1, observations = 50, p-value = 0.3297
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is not equal to 0.007968127
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.000506228 0.106469546
## sample estimates:
## frequency of outliers (expected: 0.00796812749003984 ) 
##                                                   0.02

performance

check_model(model1a, detrend=FALSE)
## `check_outliers()` does not yet support models of class `glmmTMB`.

Partial effect plots

model1a |> ggemmeans(~MASS_FEMALE) |> 
  plot() 
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

model1a |> ggemmeans(~TEMPERATURE) |> 
  plot()
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

model1a |> ggemmeans(~MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE) |> 
  plot() 

model1a |> ggemmeans(~MASS_FEMALE|TEMPERATURE) |> 
  plot()

Model investigation

Summary

model1a |> summary()
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          
## EGG_COUNT ~ scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE) * TEMPERATURE +  
##     scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center = TRUE) + (1 | FEMALE)
## Data: egg_df
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     591.5     608.7    -286.8     573.5        41 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  FEMALE   (Intercept) 4722     68.71   
##  Residual             2298     47.94   
## Number of obs: 50, groups:  FEMALE, 32
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 2.3e+03 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                                   Estimate Std. Error z value
## (Intercept)                                        464.282     27.403  16.943
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE)                   76.293     25.732   2.965
## TEMPERATURE28.5                                      9.988     37.495   0.266
## TEMPERATURE30                                      -10.588     38.532  -0.275
## scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center = TRUE)              -24.744     15.693  -1.577
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE28.5  -18.729     33.349  -0.562
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE30    -42.951     39.018  -1.101
##                                                   Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                                        < 2e-16 ***
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE)                  0.00303 ** 
## TEMPERATURE28.5                                    0.78994    
## TEMPERATURE30                                      0.78348    
## scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center = TRUE)              0.11486    
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE28.5  0.57439    
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE30    0.27099    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Anova

model1a |> Anova()

Confint

model1a |> confint()
##                                                        2.5 %     97.5 %
## (Intercept)                                        410.57397 517.989915
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE)                   25.85891 126.727664
## TEMPERATURE28.5                                    -63.50021  83.476120
## TEMPERATURE30                                      -86.10973  64.933815
## scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center = TRUE)              -55.50246   6.014399
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE28.5  -84.09235  46.634871
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE30   -119.42501  33.523865
## Std.Dev.(Intercept)|FEMALE                          49.07526  96.210621
##                                                     Estimate
## (Intercept)                                       464.281940
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE)                  76.293289
## TEMPERATURE28.5                                     9.987957
## TEMPERATURE30                                     -10.587956
## scale(MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE, center = TRUE)             -24.744032
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE28.5 -18.728737
## scale(MASS_FEMALE, center = TRUE):TEMPERATURE30   -42.950573
## Std.Dev.(Intercept)|FEMALE                         68.713616

r-squared

model1a |> r2_nakagawa()
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.761
##      Marginal R2: 0.271

post - hoc

model1a |> emmeans(~TEMPERATURE, type ="response")
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  TEMPERATURE emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  27             463 27.5 Inf       410       517
##  28.5           474 26.3 Inf       422       525
##  30             453 25.3 Inf       404       503
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
model1a |> emmeans(~TEMPERATURE, type ="response") |> pairs() |> summary() 
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions

Summary figure

egg_df <- egg_df |> drop_na(EGG_COUNT, MASS_FEMALE, MEDIAN_EGG_SIZE)
egg.emm <- emmeans(model1a, ~ MASS_FEMALE*TEMPERATURE, 
                 at =list(MASS_FEMALE=seq(from =min(egg_df$MASS_FEMALE), to =max(egg_df$MASS_FEMALE), by=.25)))

eggcount.df <- as.data.frame(egg.emm)

eggcount.obs <- egg_df |> 
  mutate(Pred =predict(model1a, re.form=NULL, type ='response'), 
         Resid =residuals(model1a, type ='response'), 
         Fit =Pred+Resid) 

eggcount.obs.summarize <-  eggcount.obs |> 
  group_by(FEMALE, TEMPERATURE) |> 
  summarise(mean.eggcount =mean(Fit, na.rm=TRUE), 
            mean.mass.female =mean(MASS_FEMALE, na.rm = TRUE), 
            min.eggcount =min(Fit, na.rm=TRUE), 
            max.eggcount =max(Fit, na.rm=TRUE),
            sd.eggcount =sd(Fit, na.rm =TRUE), 
            n.eggcount = n()) |> 
  mutate(se.eggcount = sd.eggcount / sqrt(n.eggcount), 
         lower.ci.eggcount =mean.eggcount - qt(1-(0.05/2), n.eggcount -1) * se.eggcount, 
         upper.ci.eggcount =mean.eggcount + qt(1-(0.05/2), n.eggcount -1) * se.eggcount) |> 
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by FEMALE and TEMPERATURE.
## ℹ Output is grouped by FEMALE.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(FEMALE, TEMPERATURE))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
eggcount.plot <- ggplot(data = eggcount.df, aes(x=MASS_FEMALE, y=emmean)) + 
  stat_smooth(aes(color=TEMPERATURE), 
              method = "lm") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=asymp.LCL, ymax=asymp.UCL, fill=TEMPERATURE), alpha=0.5) +
  geom_pointrange(data = eggcount.obs.summarize, aes(x =mean.mass.female, 
                                                  y =mean.eggcount, 
                                                  ymin =mean.eggcount -sd.eggcount, 
                                                  ymax =mean.eggcount + sd.eggcount, 
                                                  color = TEMPERATURE)) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values =c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1000), breaks=seq(0, 1000, 100)) +
  facet_wrap(~TEMPERATURE)+
  xlab("MATERNAL MASS (g)") + 
  ylab("Clutch size") + 
  ggtitle("Maternal-clutch size relationship") +
  theme_classic() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

eggcount.density <- ggdensity(eggcount.obs, x="Fit", 
          color = "TEMPERATURE", 
          fill = "TEMPERATURE", 
          rug=TRUE, 
          add="median", 
          alpha=0.5,
          palette=c("#69d7d8","#ff9c56", "#903146")) + 
          clean_theme() + rotate() + 
  theme(legend.position = c(0.7,0.9), 
                                legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "black", size=1.5))

eggcount.plot.final <-eggcount.plot + eggcount.density + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect" & theme(legend.position = "bottom"), 
              widths = c(2,1)); eggcount.plot.final
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# delete below # slopes

variation among clutches

cv_among <- eggcount.obs |> 
  distinct(FEMALE, .keep_all=TRUE) |> 
  group_by(TEMPERATURE) |> 
  mutate(mean_among =mean(Fit), 
         sd_among =sd(Fit), 
         cv_among =(sd_among/mean_among)*100) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  distinct(cv_among, .keep_all = T) |> 
  select(c("TEMPERATURE","cv_among", "mean_among","sd_among")) |> 
  arrange(as.numeric(TEMPERATURE))
cv_among
